_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites 'text' from live numbers; these are the computed fallbacks so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.cape: The Shiller CAPE ratio stands at 40.7 in June 2026, more than double its 140-year average of 17.3 and close to the all-time high of 44.19 from December 1999. Such elevated valuation measures have historically signaled market bubbles.
notes.buffett: The Buffett Indicator shows the total market capitalization at 231.7% of GDP, far exceeding the dot-com peak of 146% and the 2007 peak of 110%. This suggests stocks may be significantly overvalued relative to the economy.
notes.concentration: Market concentration is intense, with the top 5 stocks making up nearly 30% of the S&P 500, and the tech sector accounting for 48%. The top 10 holdings now represent 41% of the index, up from 17.5% in 2015, heightening systemic risk.
notes.yieldcurve: The yield curve spread between 10-year and 3-month Treasuries is just 0.55 points, while the NY Fed estimates a 31% chance of recession. This flat curve combined with recession risks often precedes market downturns.
notes.drypowder: Berkshire Hathaway holds a record $397.4 billion in cash, but 79% of institutions expect liquidity to tighten, meaning the abundant dry powder could soon dry up and limit buying support.
notes.finale: The trigger window from December 2026 to May 2027 features IPO lock-up expirations for giants like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic, likely releasing shares into the market and draining liquidity just as investor cash reserves are expected to decline.
